name: Chiayi City (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the spirit and history of the city

description: A navy-blue field charged with a layered emblem in white, green and red, depicting an abstract sun, mountains and stylised foliage representing Alishan tea, with a Chinese-character motif at the top.
history: Chiayi City (Jiayi) is one of Taiwan's three provincial cities, separated administratively from the surrounding Chiayi County. The current city flag bears an emblem with multiple layers symbolising the city's setting at the foot of the Alishan mountains, its tea-growing heritage and its identity as 'the city of pictures' (Hua Cheng) — a name dating from the Qing era.

features.symbolism: layered composition representing the Alishan mountain range, the tea-growing slopes around the city, and the stylised character 嘉 (jia, 'celebrated', from 嘉義/Chiayi)
